text.title: Multimodal VUI Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the multimodal-vui-design methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each feature MUST use exactly one of four interaction patterns: voice-initiated/screen-completed, screen-initiated/voice-completed, voice+visual feedback, voice-nav/visual-content.
rule.rationale: Mixing patterns within a feature causes state-sync bugs.

rule.statement: Feature MUST document a fallback hierarchy: voice → touch → keyboard.
rule.rationale: Voice fails in noise; touch fails for hand-busy users; keyboard is always-available.

rule.statement: Every voice prompt MUST define timeout behaviour + error-state UI.
rule.rationale: Without explicit timeouts users wait silently / restart.

rule.statement: State-sync between voice intent and screen UI MUST be verified with an automated test before ship.
rule.rationale: Voice-screen drift is the dominant smart-display bug class.

rule.statement: Voice prompts MUST be localised + tested for locale-specific phrasings.
rule.rationale: English-only voice on a Spanish device fails ASR.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
